channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance

item.title: Microbial metabolite Urolithin A protects against inorganic arsenic-induced gut barrier dysfunction in humanized AS3MT mice.
item.description: Gut microbes - Chronic exposure to inorganic arsenic (iAs) remains a major environmental health concern and is associated with significant gastrointestinal (GI) disorders, including gastroenteritis, diarrhea, and...

item.title: Dual Targeted Therapy for Refractory Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Journal of evidence-based medicine - Emerging evidence suggests that dual targeted therapy (DTT), which employs two biologics or small molecules concurrently, may improve outcomes for patients with refractory inflammatory bowel diseas...

item.title: Evolving Research Hotspots and Emerging Trends in Atopic Dermatitis and Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Bibliometric Analysis.
item.description: Clinical, cosmetic and investigational dermatology - The number of people affected by atopic dermatitis (AD) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues to rise, increasing the disease burden. These conditions are epidemiologically linked and shar...

item.title: Extensive library of Ruminococcus gnavus phages revealing genomic and phenotypic diversity with novel clusters.
item.description: Microbiology spectrum - Ruminococcus gnavus is a prevalent member of human gut microbiota and is associated with inflammatory bowel disease. Phage-based microbiota engineering represents a promising approach for alleviati...
